exemplar.title: How does the terminal velocity of a spherical object falling through a viscous fluid vary with its radius, and how can Stokes' Law be applied to predict this relationship?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the terminal velocity of a spherical object falling through a viscous fluid vary with its radius, and how can Stokes' Law be applied to predict this relationship?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs are labelled clearly with units, significant figures, and error bars, achieving both clarity and precision.
Uncertainties are propagated through distance, time, density, radius, and terminal velocity calculations.
Data processing is accurate: regression analyses and statistical tests (correlation coefficient, paired t-test) are applied correctly.
Processed empirical results are tabulated with uncertainties and percentage errors, enabling precise theoryâexperiment comparison.
weaknesses: Viscosity is treated as an exact literature value with no associated uncertainty.
Uncertainty in gravitational acceleration is omitted from propagation.
Overlapping uncertainty ranges in volume vs. radius-squared data are not discussed in terms of trend clarity or statistical significance.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication of data recording and processing is both clear and precise, and the majority of calculations are accurate. Some significant omissions in uncertainty consideration (viscosity, g) warrant placement at the top of the 3-4 band for uncertainties but overall top-band performance in communication and processing justifies a score of 5.

strengths: Research question is situated within a specific engineering and environmental context, clearly linking terminal velocity and Stokesâ Law to seed dispersal.
Rationale robustly connects fluid dynamics theory to a practical droneâbased scenario, demonstrating deep understanding.
Methodological considerations are well explained, justifying controlled variables and laminar flow requirements in terms of Stokesâ Law.
Procedure is organized and sequential, with materials list and photos enabling reproduction with only minor ambiguities.
weaknesses: Exact fluid depth is not specified, reducing full procedural reproducibility.
Inconsistency in number of marbles referenced (five vs. seven) could confuse replication.
Assumption of laminar flow is stated without Reynolds number calculation or validation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall performance in research design is strong: the research question is contextualized at the highest level, and methodological considerations are largely explained. Minor procedural omissions (fluid height, marble count) prevent full unambiguous reproduction, placing this work solidly in the 5-6 band but meriting one mark below the maximum.

strengths: Conclusion directly answers the research question, citing the strong linear correlation and quantifying deviations via regression slope and p-value.
Comparison of empirical vs. theoretical uncertainty ranges (1.8â6.7% vs. 5â16.3%) adds scientific context.
Conclusion is entirely consistent with the preceding analysis, demonstrating full justification.
weaknesses: Depth of comparison to accepted scientific context is somewhat limited (no quantitative Reynolds number discussion).
No combined table directly contrasting theoretical and experimental velocities with uncertainties, which could streamline the conclusion.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully supported by the analysis and justifies the findings with relevant data, placing it in the top band for relevance and support. The comparison to scientific context, while relevant, lacks some depth, indicating a mark at the lower end of the 5-6 band.

strengths: Evaluation identifies specific methodological weaknesses (viscosity assumption, wall effects, rotation) and links each to potential impact.
Suggested improvements are realistic and directly address the identified weaknesses (viscometer, larger flask, high-speed camera).
Tables clearly align each limitation with a corresponding practical improvement.
weaknesses: Relative impact of each methodological weakness (magnitude of error) is not fully explained.
Improvements are described but not elaborated quantitatively in terms of how they would enhance accuracy.
Some suggestions lack discussion of implementation feasibility or costâbenefit analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes specific weaknesses and offers relevant, realistic improvements, placing it in the 3-4 band. However, it stops short of explaining the relative impact or quantitatively detailing how each improvement would enhance results, meriting a solid 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is clearly stated â it specifies terminal velocity, spherical objects, viscous fluid and Stokesâ law â and it is placed in a concrete, real-world context (seed dispersal from drones, relevance to engineering and environmental science).  This is more than a broad backdrop; it is a specific scenario that justifies why the relationship is worth investigating, satisfying the descriptor for the top band.

Uncertainties are propagated for distance, time, density, radius and terminal velocity and percentage uncertainties are reported.  However, viscosity is taken as an exact literature value (no uncertainty), and uncertainty in g is ignored; thus consideration is substantial but with significant omissions, placing the work at the top of the 3-4 band.

The student not only lists controlled variables but also explains why each one matters for collecting relevant and sufficient data; justification is linked to Stokesâ law and fluid dynamics.  They outline how uncertainties, Reynolds number assumptions, and laminar flow requirements influence data quality.  This constitutes an explanation rather than a simple description, meeting the 5-6 band, though occasional gaps (viscosity not measured) warrant awarding the lower mark of 5.

A detailed table identifies specific weaknesses (viscosity assumption, wall effects, rotation) and explains how each could influence results (e.g. over-prediction of velocity).  The impact is discussed but not always in terms of relative magnitude; hence description rather than full explanation, aligning with the 3-4 bandâs upper mark.

The step-by-step method, materials list, and photographs allow another student to reproduce the investigation with only minor ambiguities (e.g. the number of marbles is inconsistent, exact fluid height not stated).  Overall the procedure is organised and sequential, but those small omissions prevent a completely unambiguous replication, so the strand fits best at the top of the 3-4 band.

Calculations follow the stated formulas, terminal velocities are derived consistently, linear regressions are produced, and statistical tests (r, paired t-test) are applied correctly.  No major inaccuracies are evident, so processing quality is appropriate and accurate, meriting the top bandâs lower mark.

For every limitation a realistic improvement is suggested (measure viscosity with viscometer, wider flask, high-speed camera).  Suggestions are relevant and go beyond a simple statement, yet they are not elaborated in depth on how they would quantitatively enhance results, so the strand sits comfortably at the top of the 3-4 band.

Raw-data tables have clear titles, units and significant figures; processed tables distinguish empirical vs theoretical values; graphs are labelled with axes, units, error bars and regression equations.  Communication is both clear (easy to follow) and precise (appropriate sig. figs. and uncertainties stated), fully satisfying the top band.

Stokesâ law is used as the accepted context; the student compares empirical slope to theoretical, discusses viscosity assumptions and laminar flow.  The comparison is relevant and mostly justified, yet depth is limited (e.g. no quantitative discussion of Reynolds number validity), so the strand fits best in the 3-4 band.

The conclusion directly answers the research question, cites the strong linear correlation, discusses agreement and systematic deviation, and quantifies the difference via regression slopes and p-value.  It is fully consistent with the presented analysis, fulfilling the highest descriptor.

annotations.comment: The research question is situated within a specific engineering and environmental context and clearly outlines how terminal velocity and Stokesâ Law relate to seed dispersal, demonstrating excellent contextualization.
The rationale robustly links fluid dynamics theory to a practical drone-based seed deployment scenario, strengthening the investigationâs justification and demonstrating deep understanding.
The methodological considerations are well explained, linking controlled variables, Reynolds number, and laminar flow requirements back to Stokesâ Law to justify data sufficiency.
The controlled variables table omits the exact fluid depth used. Specify and justify the fluid height to ensure full procedural reproducibility.
Methodology lists five marbles but later refers to seven in the drop trials. Resolve this inconsistency to avoid confusion and ensure accurate planning.
Raw data are presented with clear headings, units, and uncertainties, facilitating straightforward interpretation and indicating strong data communication.
The description of data processing steps is articulated clearly and precisely, with appropriate reference to uncertainty propagation formulas, demonstrating effective communication.
Viscosity is taken as a fixed literature value without uncertainty. This omission underestimates propagated errors and should be addressed by including a realistic uncertainty range.
The radius-squared uncertainty calculation includes superfluous significant digits that obscure clarity. Round to meaningful figures (e.g., two significant figures) and reflect realistic uncertainty.
Figure 3âs regression line lacks error bars on the theoretical data. Add uncertainty bars or shading to demonstrate confidence intervals.
Processed empirical data are tabulated with clear uncertainties and percentage errors, enabling precise comparison to theory and demonstrating high-quality processing.
Figure 4âs axes are labeled with units and data points include error bars, providing a clear and precise visual of empirical results.
The conclusion succinctly states that empirical results confirm the proportionality between $r^2$ and $v$, and effectively references the strong correlation coefficient.
The discussion of empirical vs. theoretical uncertainty ranges (1.8â6.7% vs. 5â16.3%) adds scientific context and demonstrates thoughtful comparison.
To strengthen evaluation, directly measure fluid viscosity with a viscometer and include its uncertainty rather than assuming a literature value.
The evaluation table identifies specific systematic and random limitations and links each to practical improvements, reflecting a thorough description of weaknesses.
The suggested improvements table presents realistic and relevant modifications, clearly linking each to specific identified limitations.
The methodology strengths table effectively justifies multiple-trial reliability and control of variables, highlighting robust aspects of the design.
One methodology strengths entry is truncated. Complete the justification sentence to maintain clarity and professionalism in the table.
The assumption of laminar flow is stated without calculation. Include an approximate Reynolds number check to substantiate the laminar flow condition.
When introducing Stokesâ Law, discuss its applicability limits explicitly (Re < 1) and the impact of deviations beyond those limits.
Supply a full citation for the IB data booklet formulas used in uncertainty propagation to strengthen academic rigour and source transparency.
Volume vs. radius-squared data show overlapping uncertainty ranges for adjacent marbles; discuss how this impacts the clarity of the trend and statistical significance.
Theoretical data uncertainties vary widely. Include discussion on why some theoretical uncertainties are large (e.g., from density variations) and how this affects model validation.
Consider presenting a combined table that directly contrasts theoretical and experimental velocitiesâwith uncertaintiesâto streamline comparison in the conclusion.
Figure 4âs caption reads only âFigure 4.â Add a descriptive title (e.g., âTerminal Velocity vs. Radius Squaredâ) to clarify the figureâs content.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
